patch.value:
## Context
Best-practice PM standards expect flow metrics — cycle time, lead time, throughput, aging WIP, cumulative flow — and pm already stores everything needed: per-item history streams carry every status transition with timestamps. Today the core surface only offers point-in-time counts (stats, aggregate); nothing computes time-in-status.

## EXTEND, do not create (per package-ecosystem convention)
The existing pm-brief package (unbraind/pm-brief: token-budgeted agent briefs and next-work plans) is the natural home — this audit confirmed via gh repo list that pm-brief, pm-gantt-chart, pm-ops (multi-repo report), and pm-context already split the reporting space, and none compute flow metrics. Extend pm-brief with a metrics section rather than shipping a new package or a new core command.

## Deliverables (scope for grooming)
1. History-derived per-item facts: entered/left timestamps per status, computed via the SDK history read primitives (pm-4a7m/pm-oslr lineage) — logic belongs in the SDK-consumable layer so other packages (pm-web, pm-ops) can reuse it.
2. Aggregations in pm-brief output: cycle/lead time percentiles by type/tag/parent over a window, throughput per week, aging-WIP list (open items by time-in-status) — token-budgeted like the rest of pm-brief.
3. Composable raw output: metrics available as JSON/NDJSON rows so grep/jq pipelines and external dashboards can consume them (pm-m9xv story).

## Relationships
- Parent pm-ugqx (package platform); consumes pm-usfg promoted history/read primitives.
- Complements pm-gantt-chart (schedule view) and pm-ops (fleet report); feeds the pm-doxj objective of best-practice PM standards.
- Horizon: mid — after the annotation/history SDK promotions expose the needed reads.

## Duplicate check (2026-07-07 audit #16)
Searched "burndown velocity report analytics", "cycle time lead time metrics", "flow metrics throughput", "pm-brief reporting metrics extend" — no existing item covers time-in-status metrics; nearest are shipped count-based aggregate/stats work and the reporting packages listed above.
Extend the existing pm-brief package with flow metrics: cycle/lead time, throughput, and aging-WIP computed from item history streams

Best-practice PM flow metrics (cycle time, lead time, throughput, aging WIP, cumulative flow) computed from the status-transition timestamps already in per-item history streams. EXTENDS the existing unbraind/pm-brief package (confirmed live via gh repo list; pm-gantt-chart/pm-ops/pm-context split the rest of the reporting space) — no new package, no new core command; metric computation lands on SDK history read primitives so pm-web/pm-ops can reuse it.

pm-brief output gains a token-budgeted metrics section with cycle/lead percentiles by type/tag/parent over a window, weekly throughput, and aging-WIP; raw metric rows available as JSON/NDJSON for jq pipelines; computation built on promoted SDK history/read primitives, not private core imports
